title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was fatally shot by New Hampshire State Police after a shootout during an attempted arrest.
Masse allegedly shot and wounded a Nottingham officer earlier that day after firing at his family members.
A shelter-in-place was lifted after Masse was killed; the incident remains under investigation with body cam footage reviewed.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon, from Canton, Mass., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon after recovering from breast cancer.
Her best friend, Peggy, also a cancer survivor, will join her to raise awareness and support Mass General.
Both women aim to celebrate life and inspire others with their resilience and friendship in the race.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'Saturday Night Live' on April 4, marking his fifth time as host.
Musical guest Jack White performed for the sixth time, including a past appearance with The White Stripes.
Black participated in a cameo-heavy five-timers sketch with notable SNL alums like Tina Fey and Jonah Hill.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledged past criticisms about his left hand, attributing struggles to a prior wrist injury.
Brown underwent major surgery in 2021 and reinjured his wrist in 2023, impacting his left-side play.
Improved health and treatment have bolstered Brown's left-side game, contributing to his career-high stats this season.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returns to Boston with the Padres, reflecting on his tenure with the Red Sox as his dream job.
Orsillo, who worked at NESN from 2001-2015, appreciates fans and former colleagues remembering him fondly.
Orsillo, now with the Padres, values his San Diego role, calling it a successful 'Plan B' after leaving NESN.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Alex Cora used Roman Anthony as designated hitter due to defensive concerns early this Red Sox season.
Anthony contributed offensively with a triple and a base hit against the Padres, aiding the Red Sox attack.
Boston Red Sox have a 2-6 record ahead of their Sunday matchup against the Padres at Fenway Park.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early walked four batters but held the Padres to two runs in the Red Sox's 3-2 loss.
Aroldis Chapman allowed the Padres' winning run in the ninth inning, giving Boston their sixth loss of the season.
Connelly Early has tied the Red Sox record with 39 strikeouts in his first six career regular season games.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated CF Montreal 3-0, with goals from Luca Langoni, Mamadou Fofana, and Peyton Miller.
Head coach Marko Mitrović was red-carded in the 34th minute for leaving his technical area during the game.
Revolution players scored from set pieces, showcasing effective strategy led by set piece coach Marc Ortí Esteban.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox’ Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas is set to resume hitting after a rib injury forced him to stop baseball activities last week.
Johan Oviedo will consult with his Tommy John surgeon after being sidelined with a right elbow strain and reduced velocity.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval are nearing minor league rehab outings after missing the 2025 season due to injuries.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, a 22-year-old cancer survivor from Dedham, is running the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Taylor was diagnosed at 19 with a rare, aggressive thyroid cancer that spread to her lymph nodes and vocal cords.
Mass General's AYA program and survivorship class have been integral in Taylor's recovery and marathon motivation.
